gate: Physical USB destructive + crash + rollback on an Apple-written HFS+ volume; Apple fsck_hfs + macOS-kernel RW mount
environment: qemu/KVM macOS Sequoia recovery (apfs/hfs kext, fsck_hfs hfs-683.100.9); OSX-KVM snap3 USB config (ICH9-LPC.disable_s3=1, ich9-EHCI + nec-usb-xhci msi=off); the disposable USB disk passed through to the VM via 'wsl --mount --bare PHYSICALDRIVE2' -> /dev/sde -> qemu ide-hd raw drive
physical_disk: Seagate Expansion Desk, serial NAAA3QVK, 7.3 TiB USB (Windows disk 2), fully disposable per user; whole-disk wipe authorized. Partitioned GPT with a 256 MiB Apple HFS (AF00) partition 'SAKH8' at sector 2048.
apple_written_base: bundled newfs_hfs (Apple diskdev_cmds-540.1 source) created a 256 MiB journaled HFS+ volume (newfs_hfs -J -v SAKH8); the macOS kernel + native fsck_hfs hfs-683.100.9 are the external Apple validators
sak_mutations: sak_hfs_writer_cli on the Apple-written volume: create-file-image /h8proof.txt (+ data fork), create-file-image /to-rename.txt, rename-catalog-entry-image /to-rename.txt -> /renamed.txt; bundled fsck_hfs clean + certifier readback Passed before writing to physical media

physical_destructive.proof: disk 2 wiped (sfdisk --wipe always) + GPT Apple HFS partition SAKH8 created + S.A.K.-mutated Apple HFS+ image dd'd to the raw partition offset (sector 2048); HFS+ signature 0x482B verified on the physical partition; GPT intact after write

kernel_rw_mount_and_read.proof: macOS kernel auto-mounted /dev/disk0s1 (SAKH8) RW (fsck refused: 'volume is mounted with write access') and ls /Volumes/SAKH8 read S.A.K.'s catalog: h8proof.txt + renamed.txt (the S.A.K.-created + renamed files). Evidence h8-physical-mount-fsck.png
kernel_rw_mount_and_read.disk_size_confirms_partition: diskutil info /dev/disk0s1 -> Disk Size 268.4 MB (268435456 Bytes) (exactly 524288 512-Byte-Units) = the exact 256 MiB SAKH8 partition

apple_native_fsck_hfs.proof: diskutil unmount force /dev/disk0s1 then fsck_hfs -fy /dev/disk0s1 (version hfs-683.100.9, macOS NATIVE, newer than bundled 540.1): Checking Journaled HFS Plus volume / extents overflow / catalog / multi-linked files / catalog hierarchy / extended attributes / volume bitmap / volume information -> 'The volume SAKH8 appears to be OK.' Evidence h8-physical-mount-fsck.png

kernel_rw_write.proof: diskutil mount /dev/disk0s1 then the macOS kernel wrote h8-crash.txt into the S.A.K.-mutated volume (echo > /Volumes/SAKH8/h8-crash.txt; sync); ls showed h8-crash.txt alongside h8proof.txt + renamed.txt. Evidence h8-physical-kernel-write.png

crash_and_journal_replay_rollback.proof: qemu killed (kill -9, simulated power loss) while SAKH8 was mounted with the kernel-written h8-crash.txt -> VM rebooted -> the macOS kernel auto-mounted disk0s1 (HFS+ journal replay recovered the unclean volume) -> h8-crash.txt SURVIVED the crash (kernel-written data persisted through the full qemu kill + reboot, proving it is the real physical disk, not a RAM copy) -> fsck_hfs -fy hfs-683.100.9 'The volume SAKH8 appears to be OK.' Evidence h8-physical-postcrash.png

fsck_hfs: Executing fsck_hfs (version hfs-683.100.9). ** Checking Journaled HFS Plus volume. ... ** The volume SAKH8 appears to be OK. (both before and after the crash)
